title: Wire extra-packages.yaml as single source of truth for Docker build packages
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for 'There is releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml. JEdoc'

Restructure releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml into pip_extras (bare extras: ldap, memcache_pool, oauth1), pip_packages (standalone packages, initially empty), and apt_packages (runtime: libapache2-mod-wsgi-py3, libldap2, libsasl2-2, libxml2) — fixing the incorrect libldap-2.5-0 to libldap2. Add a 'Resolve extra packages' step in .github/workflows/build-images.yaml (build-service-images job, after the existing 'Resolve source ref' step) that uses yq to extract all three fields per service from extra-packages.yaml and writes them to $GITHUB_OUTPUT. Pass these as --build-arg PIP_EXTRAS, PIP_PACKAGES, and EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES to docker/build-push-action. In images/keystone/Dockerfile, declare the three ARGs and replace the hardcoded [ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1] with [${PIP_EXTRAS}], add ${PIP_PACKAGES} to uv pip install, and replace the hardcoded apt package list with ${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES}. Update tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh: remove the now-obsolete test_extra_packages_match_dockerfile function and update test_extra_packages_valid_yaml_structure to validate the new field names (pip_extras, pip_packages, apt_packages), non-empty pip_extras and apt_packages, and valid package name patterns.

**Rationale:** extra-packages.yaml exists but is completely ignored by the build pipeline — packages are independently hardcoded in the Dockerfile, creating silent drift risk and a false sense of configuration. The file also contains libldap-2.5-0 which does not exist as an Ubuntu Noble package (correct name: libldap2). Making the YAML the single source of truth eliminates duplication, prevents drift, and ensures release-specific package declarations actually reach the built images.

**Affected Areas:**
- releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml
- .github/workflows/build-images.yaml
- images/keystone/Dockerfile
- tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh
elaboration:
**Size:** 🔨 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high

---

## Summary

`releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml` declares per-service pip extras and runtime apt packages but is completely ignored by the Docker build pipeline — packages are hardcoded independently in `images/keystone/Dockerfile`, and the CI workflow (`build-images.yaml`) passes zero build args. This feature makes `extra-packages.yaml` the single source of truth by restructuring it into `pip_extras` + `pip_packages` + `apt_packages` fields, having CI extract values via `yq` (following the existing `source-ref` resolution pattern), and injecting them as Docker build args. The incorrect package name `libldap-2.5-0` must be corrected to `libldap2` (the actual Ubuntu Noble package name already used in the Dockerfile).

---

## Scope

**Included:**
- Restructure `releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml`: rename `pip_packages` → `pip_extras` (bare extras names: `ldap`, `memcache_pool`, `oauth1`), add empty `pip_packages` field for standalone packages, fix `libldap-2.5-0` → `libldap2`, replace the WARNING comment with a header reflecting it is now the consumed source of truth
- Modify `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` (`build-service-images` job, lines 96–196): add a "Resolve extra packages" step after "Resolve source ref" (line 111) to extract `pip_extras`, `pip_packages`, and `apt_packages` from `extra-packages.yaml` using `yq` — following the same pattern as the existing `source-ref` step (null check, `$GITHUB_OUTPUT`); pass extracted values as `build-args` to `docker/build-push-action` (line 167)
- Modify `images/keystone/Dockerfile`: declare `ARG PIP_EXTRAS`, `ARG PIP_PACKAGES`, `ARG EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES`; replace hardcoded `[ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1]` (line 17) with `[${PIP_EXTRAS}]`; add `${PIP_PACKAGES}` as additional install targets in the same `uv pip install`; replace hardcoded apt package list (lines 31–34) with `${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES}` expansion
- Update `tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh`: remove `test_extra_packages_match_dockerfile` (no longer needed — Dockerfile no longer hardcodes packages); update `test_extra_packages_valid_yaml_structure` to validate the new schema (field names `pip_extras`, `pip_packages`, `apt_packages`; non-empty `pip_extras` and `apt_packages`; valid package name patterns)

**Excluded:**
- Build-time apt packages in `images/venv-builder/Dockerfile` (e.g., `libldap2-dev`, `libffi-dev`) — compile-time dependencies, not per-service runtime packages (YAGNI)
- Base runtime packages in `images/python-base/Dockerfile` (e.g., `python3`, `ca-certificates`) — shared across all services, not per-release (YAGNI)
- Common Python packages in `venv-builder` (e.g., `cryptography`, `pymysql`) — release-independent base layer (YAGNI)
- Local `make docker-build` support — currently a stub (`error S006`); separate feature
- Adding `extra-packages.yaml` entries for services beyond keystone — only keystone exists today

---

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph release["releases/2025.2/"]
        EP["extra-packages.yaml"]
        SR["source-refs.yaml"]
        UC["upper-constraints.txt"]
    end

    subgraph ci["CI Workflow: build-service-images"]
        REF["yq: resolve source ref"]
        YQ["yq: extract pip_extras + pip_packages + apt_packages"]
        BA["Pass as --build-arg to Docker"]
    end

    subgraph dockerfile["images/keystone/Dockerfile"]
        ARG1["ARG PIP_EXTRAS"]
        ARG2["ARG PIP_PACKAGES"]
        ARG3["ARG EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES"]
        BUILD["Stage 1: uv pip install keystone with PIP_EXTRAS + PIP_PACKAGES"]
        RUNTIME["Stage 2: apt-get install EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES"]
    end

    SR --> REF
    EP --> YQ
    REF --> BA
    YQ --> BA
    BA --> ARG1
    BA --> ARG2
    BA --> ARG3
    ARG1 --> BUILD
    ARG2 --> BUILD
    UC --> BUILD
    ARG3 --> RUNTIME
```

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant YAML as extra-packages.yaml
    participant CI as CI Workflow
    participant Docker as Dockerfile
    participant Image as Final Image

    CI->>YAML: yq '.keystone.pip_extras | join(",")'
    YAML-->>CI: ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1
    CI->>YAML: yq '.keystone.pip_packages | join(" ")'
    YAML-->>CI: (empty)
    CI->>YAML: yq '.keystone.apt_packages | join(" ")'
    YAML-->>CI: libapache2-mod-wsgi-py3 libldap2 libsasl2-2 libxml2
    CI->>Docker: --build-arg PIP_EXTRAS=ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1
    CI->>Docker: --build-arg PIP_PACKAGES=
    CI->>Docker: --build-arg EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES=libapache2-mod-wsgi-py3 libldap2 ...
    Docker->>Docker: Stage 1: uv pip install keystone[PIP_EXTRAS] PIP_PACKAGES
    Docker->>Docker: Stage 2: apt-get install EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES
    Docker-->>Image: Service image with correct packages
```

---

## Key Components

- **`releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml`**: Restructure schema — `pip_extras` (bare names: `[ldap, memcache_pool, oauth1]`), `pip_packages` (standalone packages: `[]`), `apt_packages` (runtime: `[libapache2-mod-wsgi-py3, libldap2, libsasl2-2, libxml2]`); fix `libldap-2.5-0` → `libldap2`; update header comment
- **`.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` (`build-service-images` job)**: Add "Resolve extra packages" step (after line 111) using `yq` to extract all three fields; pass as `build-args` (`PIP_EXTRAS`, `PIP_PACKAGES`, `EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES`) to `docker/build-push-action` at line 167
- **`images/keystone/Dockerfile`**: Declare three `ARG`s; replace hardcoded `[ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1]` on line 17 with `[${PIP_EXTRAS}]`; add `${PIP_PACKAGES}` to the `uv pip install` command; replace hardcoded apt list on lines 31–34 with `${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES}` shell expansion
- **`tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh`**: Remove `test_extra_packages_match_dockerfile` (lines 84–108); update `test_extra_packages_valid_yaml_structure` to validate new field names (`pip_extras`, `pip_packages`, `apt_packages`), non-empty `pip_extras` and `apt_packages`, and valid package name patterns
